title: Assistance for State, Local, and Tribal Governments
summary: This page documents federal fiscal relief programs for state, local, and Tribal governments established under COVID-19 relief legislation (American Rescue Plan and CARES Act), including funding for fiscal recovery, capital projects, homeowner assistance, rental assistance, and small business credit initiatives.
key_points: American Rescue Plan provides $350 billion in emergency funding for eligible state, local, territorial, and Tribal governments via the Coronavirus State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds
Capital Projects Fund addresses infrastructure gaps exposed by the pandemic, particularly in rural and low-income communities, including broadband internet access
Homeowner Assistance Fund allocates nearly $10 billion for states, territories, and Tribes to assist vulnerable homeowners
Emergency Rental Assistance Program provides $21.6 billion to assist households unable to pay rent and utilities due to COVID-19
State Small Business Credit Initiative provides $10 billion to fund small business credit expansion initiatives
Coronavirus Relief Fund under CARES Act provides payments to state, local, and Tribal governments for COVID-19 response
why_it_matters: This page summarizes historical COVID-era fiscal transfer programs. The funding amounts ($350B, $21.6B, $10B, $10B) represent significant federal fiscal flows to subnational governments during 2020-2021. However, this is informational content about past legislative actions rather than a current market-relevant macro catalyst.
macro_context: The programs described represent major fiscal stimulus components of the COVID-19 response. The $350 billion State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds were designed to support public health response and economic recovery. These fiscal transfers were historically significant for aggregate demand during 2020-2022 but represent completed policy actions rather than current catalysts.
important_numbers: $350 billion — Coronavirus State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds
$10 billion — State Small Business Credit Initiative
